Operation 'deleteWebhook' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original summary and description are identical and overly simplistic. They lack context about what happens after deletion (no more notifications), permanence of the action, authentication requirements, and expected response. Enhanced version clarifies the side effects and provides AI agents with better understanding of when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: Remove a webhook endpoint from the system
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a webhook endpoint identified by webhookId. Once deleted, the webhook will no longer receive event notifications. This operation is permanent and cannot be undone. Requires authentication via API key. Returns a success confirmation or error if the webhook does not exist.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and overly simplistic. They lack context about what happens after deletion (no more notifications), permanence of the action, authentication requirements, and expected response. Enhanced version clarifies the side effects and provides AI agents with better understanding of when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve return metrics and performance data
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves aggregated return analytics and metrics for the authenticated partner. Supports filtering by date range and other parameters to analyze return patterns, volumes, and performance. Returns analytics data including return counts, refund amounts, return rates, and other key performance indicators. Useful for monitoring return operations and identifying trends.
